nisba.from: Áqá Siyyid Muḥammad, the Mujtahid (of Qazvín)

review.note: kinship; son of Karbilá’í Abú-Muḥammad of Sang-Sar, brother of Muḥammad-‘Alí. Keep — distinguishes from many other Abu’l-Qásims.

review.from: Abu’l-Qásim-i-Ḵhurásání (keeper of the Tomb of Bahá’u’lláh)
review.note: role; already has nisba -i-Ḵhurásání. Gloss is a custodial role, not a place. Keep as-is.
kinship; infant son of the Báb. Keep — critical disambiguator.
kinship/namesake disambiguator (brother of Ḥájí Sulaymán Khán). Keep.

review.from: Fayṣal ibn Ḥusayn (King Feisal I of ‘Iráq)
review.note: royal title gloss; could collapse to ‘Fayṣal I (King Feisal I of ‘Iráq)’ but leave for owner — Western/Arabic naming.

review.from: G̱hulám ‘Alí, son of Ḥusayn-i-‘Arab (father of Nabíl)
review.note: kinship; father of Nabíl. Keep — key disambiguator.
role; already has nisba -i-Yazdí. Gloss is a military role. Keep as-is.

review.from: G̱hází ibn Fayṣal (King G̱hází I of ‘Iráq)
review.note: royal title gloss. Leave for owner.
kinship. Keep — disambiguates from other Hádís and from Mihdí son of Ḥujjat.

review.from: Hájí Muḥammad Taqí (registrar of the fort, Nayríz)
review.note: role + place; role (registrar) is primary, not a pure nisba. Could become Ḥájí Muḥammad-Taqíy-i-Nayrízí but role is the real disambiguator. Leave for owner.
appositive duplicates well-known title 'Imám Riḍá' (an alias). Could drop gloss → 'Imám ‘Alí-ibn-i-Músá’r-Riḍá'. Leave for owner.

review.from: Karbilá’í Mírzá Muḥammad (gatekeeper of the fort)

review.note: appositive personal name (Jahán Khánum), not place/kinship — this is her given name vs. her title Mahd-i-‘Ulyá. Keep for owner; likely an alias pairing.

review.from: Masíḥ (brother of Mullá Muḥammad-Ján, martyr of Mázindarán)
review.note: kinship + place; the kinship (brother of Mullá Muḥammad-Ján) is the primary disambiguator. Could become Masíḥ-i-Mázindarání but kinship better identifies him. Leave for owner.
date disambiguator (1268 A.H.). Keep — prevents namesake collision; Ṭihrán is martyrdom site not origin.
kinship. Keep — distinguishes from Hádí (infant son of Ḥujjat).

review.from: Mullá Aḥmad (father of the martyr son and of Mullá Mírzá Muḥammad-i-Furúghí)
Mullá Báqir (Imám of the C̱hinár-Súḵhtih quarter)
review.note: role (Imám of a quarter). Keep — namesake disambiguator vs other Mullá Báqirs.

review.from: Mullá Muḥammad (second martyr of Míyámay, idx 810)
review.note: place but idx/ordinal disambiguator vs another Míyámay martyr named Mullá Muḥammad. Owner must resolve duplicate-name collision; do NOT blindly nisba-ize (would collide). Leave for owner.

review.from: Mullá Muḥammad ibn Mullá Muḥammad (martyr of Míyámay)
review.note: patronymic already in name; could add -i-Míyámay’í but the ibn-construction is the disambiguator. Leave for owner.

review.from: Mullá Muḥammad, son of Mullá Zaynu’l-‘Ábidín (martyr of Míyámay)
review.note: kinship disambiguator among Míyámay martyrs. Keep.

review.from: Mullá Muḥammad-Ḥusayn (second martyr of Míyámay, idx 808)
review.note: ordinal/idx disambiguator vs another Míyámay Mullá Muḥammad-Ḥusayn. Leave for owner.

review.from: Mullá Mírzá Muḥammad (uncle, brother of Mullá Aḥmad)
Mullá Taqí (uncle and father-in-law of Ṭáhirih)

review.note: role; already has nisba -i-Qazvíní. Gloss is descriptive. Keep as-is.
already has nisba; gloss '(martyr)' is empty. Could drop → 'Mullá Zaynu’l-‘Ábidín-i-Yazdí'. Leave for owner.

review.from: Mullá Ḥasan, son of Mullá Muḥammad-‘Alí (officer of Zaynu’l-‘Ábidín Khán)
Mullá Ḥusayn (Bábí marksman at the masjid of Nayríz)
review.note: role; NOT Mullá Ḥusayn-i-Bus̱hrú’í. Keep — must not collide with the famous Mullá Ḥusayn.

review.from: Mullá ‘Abbás (son-in-law of Mírzá Muḥammad-Taqí of Núr)
Mullá ‘Abdu’lláh (brother of a Martyr of Tabríz)

review.note: role; no description text. Keep — likely Muḥammad Muṣṭafá al-Baghdádí. Owner may add nisba if confirmed.

place + idx disambiguator (vs Muḥammad-Ibráhím martyr of Iṣfahán and of Sang-Sar). Owner should resolve collision; nisba alone (-i-Míyámay’í) may suffice but idx hint needs human check. Leave for owner.
place + idx disambiguator (vs the two other Muḥammad-Ibráhíms). nisba -i-Sangsarí may suffice; owner to confirm collision resolution. Leave for owner.

review.from: Muḥammad-Riḍá (companion of Quddús who survived Ṭabarsí)

review.note: already has nisba + title; kinship gloss. Keep.

review.from: Muḥammad-Ḥusayn (brother of Rasúl-i-Bahnimírí, martyr of Mázindarán)
review.note: kinship + place; kinship is primary disambiguator. Could be -i-Mázindarání but brother-of identifies better. Leave for owner.
kinship. Keep — brother of Abu’l-Qásim above.

review.from: Muḥammad-‘Alí Khán (commander at the Bázár quarter, Nayríz)
Mír Abu’l-Qásim (brother of Siyyid Aḥmad of Sang-Sar)
Mír Ibráhím (martyr of Sang-Sar, brother-in-law of Siyyid Aḥmad)
review.note: place + kinship; kinship distinguishes among Sang-Sar martyrs. Could be Mír Ibráhím-i-Sangsarí but kinship adds value. Leave for owner.

review.from: Mír Mihdí (martyr of Sang-Sar, uncle of Siyyid Aḥmad)
review.note: place + kinship; same as above. Leave for owner.

review.from: Mír Muḥammad-‘Alí (father of Siyyid Aḥmad of Sang-Sar)
Mírzá Abu’l-Qásim (brother of the Báb’s wife)
Mírzá Abu’l-Qásim (relative of the governor of Nayríz)
Mírzá Abu’l-Qásim (son-in-law of Mírzá Muḥammad-Taqí of Núr)
Mírzá Abu’l-Ḥasan (son of the brother-in-law of Mullá Ḥusayn)

review.note: role; could be Mírzá Aḥmad-i-Tabrízí but he is identified by office (mujtahid) and as father of Mírzá Báqir. Leave for owner.

review.from: Mírzá Báqir (mujtahid of Tabríz, son of Mírzá Aḥmad)
Mírzá Muḥammad-Báqir (builder of the Bábíyyih; lieutenant of Quddús)
Mírzá Muḥammad-Ḥasan (philosopher of the school of Mullá Ṣadrá)
Mírzá Muḥammad-Ḥusayn (son of the brother-in-law of Mullá Ḥusayn)
Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí (Letter of the Living, martyr of S̱hayḵh Ṭabarsí)
Mírzá Nawrá (of the Sádát quarter, Nayríz)
review.note: quarter/place; the Sádát quarter is a sub-locale, not a clean nisba. Leave for owner (could be Nayrízí but loses quarter detail; paired with Mírzá ‘Alí-Riḍá of same quarter).

review.from: Mírzá Áqá Khán-i-Núrí, the I‘timádu’d-Dawlih (Grand Vizir)
review.note: title/role; already has nisba -i-Núrí and title I‘timádu’d-Dawlih. Keep as-is.
quarter/place; sub-locale not clean nisba. Leave for owner (pairs with Mírzá Nawrá).
title/role; the (governor) gloss explains Válí. Could render 'Náẓim Pás̱há, Válí of Damascus'. Leave for owner.

review.from: Qulí (bearer of the body of Mullá Ḥusayn)
review.note: deed/role. Keep — namesake disambiguator (pairs with Ḥasan, same deed).

ambiguous: description says 'martyr of Mázindarán, defender of Shaykh Ṭabarsí' (place). BUT must not collide with Rúḥu’lláh son of Varqá (separate entry). Could be Rúḥu’lláh-i-Mázindarání; flagged for owner to avoid namesake collision.
kinship. Keep — the famous boy martyr, son of Varqá.
title (an-Naqíb); already has nisba al-Gaylání. Keep as-is.
role + place; identified by office (mujtahid). Could be -i-Zanjání but role distinguishes from the martyr Siyyids of Zanján. Leave for owner.

review.from: Siyyid Abú-Ṭálib (brother of Siyyid Aḥmad of Sang-Sar)
Siyyid Abú-Ṭálib (kad-ḵhudá of the Bázár quarter, Nayríz)

review.note: place; could be Siyyid Aḥmad-i-Qazvíní but collides with namesakes (Siyyid Aḥmad of Zanján, son of Vaḥíd). Owner to confirm no collision before nisba-izing. Leave for owner.

review.from: Siyyid Javád (son of Ḥájí Mírzá Siyyid ‘Alí)

review.note: occupation + place; brother of Siyyid Murtaḍá (Seven Martyrs). Could be -i-Zanjání but description distinguishes from Siyyid Káẓim-i-Zanjání (martyred Mázindarán) — namesake risk. Leave for owner.
group membership; brother of Siyyid Káẓim. Keep — must not collide with Siyyid Murtaḍá martyr of Zanján.

review.from: Siyyid Muḥammad-Riḍá (brother of Siyyid Aḥmad of Sang-Sar)

review.note: already has nisba; gloss is a role. NOTE: distinct from the antichrist Siyyid Muḥammad-i-Iṣfahání — owner must verify this is the Ṭabarsí companion, not collide. Keep/flag.
place; could be Siyyid Mírzá-i-Mans̱hádí. But 'Mírzá' here may be part of given name; owner to confirm before nisba-izing. Leave for owner.

review.from: Siyyid Riḍá (companion of Quddús, envoy to the prince)

review.note: kinship; brother of the Báb's amanuensis. Keep.
role + place; identified as a notable. Could be -i-Nayrízí but namesake risk with the amanuensis Siyyid Ḥusayn-i-Yazdí and other Ḥusayns. Leave for owner.
role + place. Could be -i-Káshání but role distinguishes. Leave for owner.

review.from: Siyyid ‘Alí Khán (host of ‘Azíz Khán at Zanján)

review.note: named only by patronymic 'Son of Íṭábakí-C̱húpán' — no given name. Keep — cannot nisba-ize a kinship-only label.
role + place; the great Najaf ecclesiastic (S̱hayḵh Muḥammad-Ḥasan, author of Jawáhir). Could be -i-Najafí but office is primary. Leave for owner.
role + place; Vaḥíd's father-in-law. Could be -i-Nayrízí but role/kinship is the identifier. Leave for owner.
kinship. Keep — son of the great S̱hayḵh Aḥmad.
occupation + place; mason native of Kirmán. Could be Ustád Zaynu’l-‘Ábidín-i-Kirmání but occupation is part of identity. Leave for owner.

review.from: William I (Wilhelm I), Emperor of Germany, King of Prussia
William II (Wilhelm II), Emperor of Germany
Yaḥyá Khán (father of Ḥájí Sulaymán Khán)
review.note: kinship. Keep — disambiguates from Farruḵh Khán's father.

review.from: the Minister of War (deported to Káshán)
the Niẓámu’l-Mulk (son of Mírzá Áqá Khán-i-Núrí)
the ambitious Armenian (founder of the “Scientific Society”)
review.note: anonymous role descriptor, no name. Keep.

review.from: the brother of Siyyid Murtaḍá (martyr of Ṭabarsí)
review.note: kinship-only label, no given name. Keep.
office + date, no personal name. Keep.

review.from: the sister of the Minister of War (hostess to Ṭáhirih)
the siyyid of Yazd (martyr of Bárfurús̱h)
review.note: anonymous descriptor; 'siyyid of Yazd' is itself the descriptor not a name. Keep.

review.from: Áqá Siyyid ‘Alí (maternal uncle of the Báb)
review.note: kinship. Keep — guardian/uncle of the Báb.

review.from: Áqá S̱hayḵh Hádí (uncle of the wife of Vaḥíd)

review.note: title + office; King's maternal uncle. Keep.
place; BUT pairs with 'Son of Íṭábakí-C̱húpán' (kept) — nisba-izing the father risks confusing the pair. Could be -i-Savád-Kúhí; owner to confirm. Leave for owner.

review.from: Ḥasan (bearer of the body of Mullá Ḥusayn)
review.note: deed/role. Keep — namesake disambiguator (pairs with Qulí).
kinship + age. Keep — distinguishes from other sons of Ḥujjat.
already has nisba; kinship gloss. Keep.

review.from: Ḥájí Siyyid Asadu’lláh (son of Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir)

review.note: role + place; the eminent Iṣfahán mujtahid. Could be -i-Iṣfahání but office is primary identifier. Leave for owner.
place; could be Ḥájí Zaynu’l-‘Ábidín-i-Kinár-Girdí. Kinár-Gird is a small waystation; owner to confirm nisba form. Leave for owner.

review.from: Ḥájí ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb (disciple of S̱hayḵh Aḥmad in Yazd)
review.note: role; man of Yazd. Could be -i-Yazdí but description is discipleship not origin. Leave for owner.

review.from: Ṣafar-‘Alí (martyr of Sang-Sar, son of Karbilá’í ‘Alí)
review.note: place + kinship; kinship distinguishes. Could be -i-Sangsarí but son-of adds value. Leave for owner.

review.from: ‘Abbás Mírzá, the Náyibu’s-Salṭanih (crown prince, father of Muḥammad Sháh)
review.note: title + kinship; already has title Náyibu’s-Salṭanih. Keep.
already has nisba; kinship gloss. Keep.
pen-name appositive (Ávárih); he is the apostate Áyatí. Keep.
place + kinship fragment; the gloss '(Yazd, brother)' is malformed — one of two brothers beheaded at Yazd (with Muḥammad-Ḥasan). Owner should clean up; possibly ‘Alí-Aṣg̱har-i-Yazdí. Leave for owner.
